items.rationale: Believes core crypto coordination should extend to physical services — energy grids, transportation, wireless — where verification cost is below incumbent margin. A repeated speaking/thesis topic through 2023.

citations.quote: We should have a decentralized energy grid, transportation network, and wireless network
An honor to speak at @FundingCommons about DePIN (decentralized physical infrastructure networks).
The essential question when considering building a DePIN network: Verification cost < incumbent margin

items.rationale: Sees the future of ML as decentralized; backed Gensyn and repeatedly explored storing LLMs on decentralized storage with on-chain inference proofs.

citations.quote: We're thrilled to be working with @harrygrieve, @benfielding and the team at @gensynai. The future of machine learning is decentralized
store an LLM on Filecoin/Arweave; prove inference with validity proofs posted on Ethereum

items.rationale: Tracks proving-system performance closely (JOLT/Lasso, SNARK overhead) and champions privacy as a frontier alongside scaling; promoted Aleo and Aztec.

citations.quote: Shocking work to wrangle SNARK performance forward. Another must read banger from @SuccinctJT!
Even more scaling (believe it or not!), privacy, interoperability.
The next generation of Aztec is on the horizon, check out how privacy can work on Ethereum!

items.rationale: Wants to reframe mechanism design + cryptography for coordination beyond financial 'tokenomics'; published two pieces and actively recruits builders working on non-financial token applications.

citations.quote: I would suggest we rebrand the study of using mechanism design and cryptography to solve coordination problems as “Tokenology.”
Tokens matter because they allow builders to keep the high dimensionality inherent in value representations and expand the design space

items.rationale: Explicitly argues infrastructure is under-focused relative to apps; frames crypto as the substrate for human coordination 'at the pace of software'.

citations.quote: I actually think we don’t have enough focus on crypto infrastructure relative to apps.
Building the rails for the future of human coordination seems like a massive opportunity and there is a lot to do

items.rationale: Views on-chain lending as an essential DeFi primitive; sustained multi-year support of Morpho's peer-to-peer matching as a Pareto rate improvement.
items.age_note: Thesis first stated 2022-07, restated as recently as 2023-10-10, so current not stale.

citations.quote: Big day for trustless lending! It's been an honor to work with @PaulFrambot and the @MorphoLabs team
We believe on-chain lending is an essential DeFi primitive

items.rationale: Backs Pimlico and is actively probing best new-user mobile wallet UX with cloud-sharded seed backups — signals conviction in better onboarding via AA.

citations.quote: It's been a privilege working with @kristofgazso and @pimlicoHQ, read more below!
What's the best mobile wallet user experience today setting up a new wallet from scratch?

items.rationale: Considers permissionless, retroactively-compensated contribution essential to DAO success; open question is strategyproof post-hoc valuation of contributions.

citations.quote: I'm very excited about the idea of permissionless contribution.
anyone can contribute to a protocol or DAO and receive compensation in proportion to the value you created

items.rationale: Fascinated by convergence of Ethereum and Cosmos roadmaps (rollups, shared sequencers, mesh security/restaking); sees rollup stacks as the new Cosmos SDK.

citations.quote: [OP Stack, ZK Stack, Orbit, Rollkit] is the new Cosmos SDK!
Honor to talk @NebularBuilders about the collision in Ethereum and Cosmos roadmaps

items.rationale: Probing whether winner-take-all network effects form around solvers coordinating inclusion auctions for app-specific and generalized intents.

citations.quote: Will there be a winner take all dynamic or network effect around solvers participating on underlying protocols?

items.rationale: Worries that centralization at the network layer lets DNS providers and ISPs censor large portions of crypto networks — a structural vulnerability beneath app-layer decentralization.
items.age_note: Stated once on 2022-07-24 and not restated; persisted concern, ~18 months old.

citations.quote: centralization at the network level that would allow DNS providers and ISPs to censor large portions of most crypto networks

items.rationale: Sarcastically frames incumbents preferring regulatory capture over genuinely scary decentralized networks — signals concern that regulation entrenches centralized incumbents.

citations.quote: nooo babe I love your regulatory capture the decentralized networks scare me

items.rationale: Argues 'MEV' conflates user-harming extraction with neutral/helpful protocol actions; the framing obscures which value extraction actually hurts users.

citations.quote: MEV is a bad term because it includes both actions that hurt users, and actions that are neutral or helpful for users

items.rationale: Repeatedly stresses decentralization is always less efficient than centralization; the cost must be justified per use case, and the cyclical pull toward centralized protocols 'creates hard times'.

citations.quote: Decentralization is always less efficient than centralization. Why is this cost worth paying, and who will bear it?
good times create centralized protocols; centralized protocols create hard times

items.rationale: Concerned that meaningful terms (DAO, modular, sovereign, zk) become contested empty labels — echoing how 'blockchain' was misapplied to databases, eroding analytical clarity.

citations.quote: modular is a scissor label; sovereign is a scissor label; zk is a scissor label
DAO seems to be one of the scissor label ... casualties of recent times

items.rationale: Personally entered a 'Solana Saga arc' using Solana Mobile and soliciting native dapp recommendations — most recent statement in corpus, signaling fresh hands-on enthusiasm.
items.age_note: Most recent dated statement (2023-12-18); reflects current posture.

citations.quote: I’m in my Solana Saga arc. Give me recommendations for native dapps or great experiences on @solanamobile

items.rationale: Interested in Filecoin's massive committed capacity as a potential data-availability layer with FVM verifying proofs, pending latency/erasure-coding solutions.

citations.quote: Anyone experimenting with Filecoin as a data availability layer? ... Filecoin has massive amounts of committed capacity

risk_regime.rationale: Consistently builder-optimistic and forward-leaning on crypto infrastructure — 'indulge optimism', ETH Denver a 'whitepill', and active sourcing/backing of new networks (Gensyn, Pimlico, Morpho). Posture is structural long-horizon conviction in decentralization rather than tactical market-cycle timing; he expresses no price/trading views, and his enthusiasm is tempered by clear-eyed acknowledgment of decentralization's efficiency costs.

notes: Wuollet writes as an infrastructure-focused investor and thesis-builder, not a trader: the corpus contains essentially no token-price or macro market-timing calls, so tokens_excited/concerned are sparse and inferred from project/usage enthusiasm rather than asset views. Strongest, most recurring convictions are DePIN, decentralized AI/ML, ZK/privacy, and token design ('Tokenology'). Recency weighting: latest signals (Dec 2023 Solana, Nov 2023 Pimlico, Oct 2023 Morpho) reinforce rather than reverse earlier stances — no material reversals detected. The network-level censorship concern is the oldest unrepeated view (persisted, ~18 months). Many entries reflect open questions he is actively exploring (intents/solvers, permissionless-contribution valuation) rather than settled positions.
